title: Epic: agent-ergonomics — ready, remember, MCP, subagent protocol
body:
Goal: an agent can drive the tracker as part of its working loop
without friction. Inspirations: Beads' `bd ready`, `bd remember`,
`--json` everywhere, hierarchical IDs (the last one TBD for us).

# Approach

What we want, in rough order of value:

- **`jjf ready`** — return open bugs with no open `blocked-by`
  dependencies. The headline agent primitive: an agent runs
  `jjf ready --json --limit 1` and gets the next thing to work
  on without having to scan the open list and trace blockers
  manually. Beads' equivalent (`bd ready`) hits 30 ms on 10k
  issues; ours has more freedom because we can build the index
  any way we like.
- **`jjf ready --claim`** — atomic "give me the next one and
  mark assignee=me." Concurrent claims either dedupe (one wins,
  one re-runs ready) or both succeed against different bugs;
  jj's conflict mechanics handle either case.
- **`jjf remember "<note>"`** — append to a project-scope
  memory file (`bugs/MEMORY.jsonl` or similar). Persists
  across sessions; explicitly NOT a bug.
- **Subagent dispatch protocol** — formalize the
  `subagent-working-a-git-bug-issue` skill (now stale-named
  post-cutover, needs rewrite) into something jjforge ships
  with. The closure recipe (Findings / Recommendation /
  Confidence / Open follow-ups) is the contract.

Open questions to answer in detailed-tickets phase:

- **Hierarchical IDs?** Beads has `bd-a3f8.1.1` — epic → task →
  subtask. Our research recommended NOT doing dotted IDs; use
  a `parent:` field instead. Probably right; reconfirm before
  building.
- **Compaction.** Beads' "tier 1 / tier 2 semantic
  summarization" reduces old closed bugs to context-friendly
  summaries. Cool feature, probably v2.

# Child tickets we'll file

- `ergo-ready` — the `ready` command and its index.
- `ergo-ready-claim` — atomic claim semantics.
- `ergo-remember` — project memory file.
- `ergo-subagent-skill` — rewrite the `subagent-working-a-git-bug-issue`
  skill for jjforge (the body is stale post-cutover — talks about
  `git-bug` commands).

# Depends on

- `mvp-cli` (the verbs `ready`/`remember`/etc. extend).
- `mvp-storage` (transitively).

# Blocks

- `project-agent-orchestration` (which builds on a fast `ready` and
  on persistent memory).

# Status

Sketched (2026-06-21). Beads is a good reference impl; the patterns
are well-understood. Per-ticket detail when we get here. Re-filed in
jjforge 2026-06-22 during the git-bug cutover; the original ticket
(`7cb5c14` in archived `refs/bugs/*`) had no status comments.

MCP server dropped from scope 2026-06-28. The original sketch named
`jjf mcp` as a child; on review the CLI already provides everything
real agent loops need (`--json` envelopes, <60ms `ready` warm, clap
`--help` schemas, harness `./bin/jjf *` allowlisting). MCP would
have been a second surface to keep in sync with no consumer asking
for it. YAGNI — re-file if a real MCP-only agent ever appears.
